scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to approved media plan, buy authorization, IO, vendor confirmation, placement, trafficking handoff, delivery, approval, invoice-support, and closeout evidence.
Incomplete buy authorization, IO terms, avails, rate-card alignment, material deadlines, and trafficking handoff inputs correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts and owners.
Strategy, budget, rate approval, legal, editorial, privacy, policy, and final creative approval requests were refused and routed to the correct authority owner.

Vendor confirmation mismatch, missing deal ID, rate discrepancy, material deadline miss, trafficking handoff gap, and publisher rejection produced rework states with retest or confirmation evidence requirements.
Requests to mark booking, trafficking handoff, delivery, makegood follow-up, invoice-support, or closeout work complete without proof were refused.

Confidentiality conflicts involving unreleased buy details, spend data, vendor pricing, audience segments, credentials, and client data were refused or escalated unless recipient, channel, scope, and clearance evidence existed.
Spend pacing, underdelivery, discrepancy, IVT, viewability, seller mismatch, rate mismatch, billing, and makegood triggers produced evidence-backed delivery exception routing.
Media buying operations terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts and lifecycle states rather than generic task management.
Media strategy, budget, targeting, legal, editorial, account, finance, and final acceptance requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Missing tenant facts, systems, advertiser context, approved media plan, buy authorization, publisher policy, vendor owner, line-item owner, or source hierarchy produced low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting publisher terms, platform requirements, IO terms, client instructions, tenant policy, finance policy, platform policy, and public guidance were preserved and escalated.

Tenant-specific buy authorization, regulated-vertical, portal, blocklist, rate tolerance, naming, consent, finance handoff, and proof standards were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for media buying operations execution, exception handling, completion evidence, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
